oneOf.description: A score on a `[min, max]` scale.

JudgeValue.description:
The raw value a judge returns: a boolean or a number, matching the query kind.
Deserialized untagged from the provider's `value` field.

JudgeVerdict.description:
A judge verdict: the raw value (bool or number) plus the stated reason. Part
of onejudge's versioned [`Report`](crate::Report) contract, so it round-trips
through serde.

usage.description: Cost/token usage for the judge call, if reported.

Message.description: A single turn in the conversation.

events.description:
The normalized tool events the skill took producing this turn (assistant
turns only, and only when the harness exposed a tool transcript). Empty
otherwise. Surfaced for post-hoc analysis, streamed live, and rendered
into the transcript the judge sees.

NamedVerdict.description:
A judge verdict paired with the criterion it scored and the kind of
judgement, so a serialized report is self-describing.

criterion.description: The plain-English criterion that was scored.

kind.description: Whether it was a boolean or numeric judgement.

verdict.description: The verdict itself (value, reason, and per-call usage).

oneOf.description: The (real or simulated) user driving the skill.
System-level framing, if a provider surfaces it.

ToolEvent.description:
One normalized tool-call / action event the skill took during a turn, lifted
from `oneharness`'s `events` array (its `--events` output). Harness-agnostic,
so a consumer can inspect shell commands, file edits, and tool uses across any
harness, not just the final text. `input` is the structured, tool-shaped args
so a consumer can match on the command string or file path without re-parsing.

`input` is free-form JSON, so `Message`/`Transcript` are `PartialEq` but not
`Eq`.

index.description: Position within the run, so ordering ("did X before Y") is expressible.

input.description: Structured tool arguments (the command, the file path); `None` when none.

kind.description: `tool_call` (the skill invoked a tool) or `tool_result` (the observation).

name.description:
Normalized tool name where knowable (e.g. `bash`, `edit_file`); `None` for
a `tool_result` or when the harness did not name it.

output.description: The result/observation text, when the transcript exposed it.

messages.description: The turns in order, oldest first.

Usage.description:
Token / cost usage for one provider call, or a running total.

Each field is independently optional because not every harness reports every
signal (cost is commonly absent on subscription auth, and cache counts only
when the harness surfaces prompt-cache reads/writes). `None` means "no
signal", never "zero" — so a total stays `None` until something reports a real
number, then accumulates.

cache_read_tokens.description:
Prompt tokens served from the provider's prompt cache — a cheap read of a
previously-written prefix — when the harness reports them. `None` when the
harness does not surface cache counts, never `0` as a guess.

cache_write_tokens.description:
Prompt tokens written to the provider's prompt cache (a.k.a. cache
creation), when the harness reports them. `None` when not surfaced.

cost_usd.description: Total cost in USD, when reported.

report.description:
A judged run: the transcript, the verdicts scored against it, aggregated
usage, and whether a streaming sink short-circuited the run — stamped with the
[`SCHEMA_VERSION`] of the contract that produced it.

Build one from an [`Outcome`](crate::Outcome) with
[`Outcome::into_report`](crate::Outcome::into_report), or directly with
[`Report::new`].

assessment.description: A free-text judgement requested by the caller, if any.

completion_reason.description: Why the per-turn supervisor declared the task complete, if it did.

schema_version.description: The contract version this report was serialized under.

stopped_early.description: Whether a streaming sink asked to short-circuit the run.

transcript.description: The full conversation transcript, with tool events on assistant turns.

usage.description: Aggregated usage across every provider call (`None` if nothing reported).

verdicts.description: The verdicts scored against the transcript, in the order they were added.

report.title: Report

EvalConfig.description: One eval scored against the finished transcript.

kind.description: Boolean (a pass/fail verdict) or numeric (a score on a scale).

scale.description:
The inclusive `[min, max]` scale for a numeric eval; ignored (and rejected)
for a boolean one. Defaults to `[0, 10]`.

oneOf.description: A score on a `[min, max]` scale.

ProviderConfig.description:
Which backend runs the harness. A flat, strict struct (rather than an
internally-tagged enum, which serde cannot pair with `deny_unknown_fields`):
[`ProviderConfig::resolve`] checks that only the chosen `kind`'s fields are set
and turns it into a validated [`ProviderSpec`], so a misplaced-but-spelled-right
key (e.g. `bin` under `kind: command`) is still a loud error.

judge.description: `split`: the backend that judges and plays the simulated user.

judge_config.description:
`oneharness`: the oneharness config file the judge / simulated user run
under, passed as `oneharness run --config <path>` (default
`oneharness.judge.toml`). This is where the judge-side harness/model
selection lives.

skill.description: `split`: the backend that runs the agent's turns.

ProviderKind.description:
The provider backends the CLI can build. One enum is the single source for
both the YAML `kind:` (via `Deserialize`) and the `--provider` flag (via
clap's `ValueEnum`), so the two surfaces cannot drift.

oneOf.description: Shell out to the `oneharness` CLI (the default).
A custom command speaking the JSON-lines protocol.
Compose a skill-runner with a separate judge / simulated-user backend.

UserConfig.description: The simulated user that supervises the agent and drives the loop.

done_when.description:
A plain-English completion condition; when the judge decides it holds, the
loop ends. Without it the loop ends at `max_turns` or when the agent
declares itself done.

max_turns.description: The assistant-turn cap for this run.

persona.description: How the simulated user behaves (their instructions).

run_config.description:
The whole YAML config for one run. Field defaults let a minimal file (just a
`task`) work, while `deny_unknown_fields` makes a typo'd key a hard error
instead of a silently-ignored setting.

assessment.description: Optional prompt for a free-text judgement of the finished transcript.

evals.description: Optional criteria to score the finished transcript with.

provider.description:
Which backend runs the harness (and judges / plays the user).

Harness/model **selection** is no longer a onejudge concern: the agent side
uses oneharness's discovered `oneharness.toml`, and the judge side uses the
`provider.judge_config` file (default `oneharness.judge.toml`). Scaffold both
with `onejudge init`.

session.description:
The caller-owned session name threaded across turns; defaults to
`onejudge`.

skill.description:
Path to a **skill** directory (containing a `SKILL.md`) whose instruction
body seeds the system prompt. Resolved relative to the config file's
directory (or the working dir for a flag-only run). Optional — combine it
with `system_prompt`, use either alone, or neither.

system_prompt.description:
Extra system-prompt text for the harness. When a `skill` is also set, this
comes **first** and the skill's body is appended after it.

task.description:
The task to drive to completion. May instead be supplied by `--task`
(`-` reads stdin); required by the time the plan is built.

user.description:
The simulated user / supervisor that drives the loop. Omit for a
single-turn run (the agent answers once).

run_config.title: Config

ToolEvent.description:
One normalized tool-call / action event the skill took during a turn, lifted
from `oneharness`'s `events` array (its `--events` output). Harness-agnostic,
so a consumer can inspect shell commands, file edits, and tool uses across any
harness, not just the final text. `input` is the structured, tool-shaped args
so a consumer can match on the command string or file path without re-parsing.

`input` is free-form JSON, so `Message`/`Transcript` are `PartialEq` but not
`Eq`.

index.description: Position within the run, so ordering ("did X before Y") is expressible.

input.description: Structured tool arguments (the command, the file path); `None` when none.

kind.description: `tool_call` (the skill invoked a tool) or `tool_result` (the observation).

name.description:
Normalized tool name where knowable (e.g. `bash`, `edit_file`); `None` for
a `tool_result` or when the harness did not name it.

output.description: The result/observation text, when the transcript exposed it.

stream_event.description:
One streamed tool event delivered live to an [`Engine::run_streaming`] sink,
tagged with the turn it belongs to.
stream_event.title: StreamEvent
